title: fix: prevent race condition during database initialization by using INSERT OR IGNORE.
body:
fix: prevent race condition during database initialization by using INSERT OR IGNORE.
Fixes #27942 

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:

for more information.

- [ ] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Since I'm not a maintainer, I just wanted to suggest that adding a test could help ensure this change properly addresses the race condition.
Added test to check if modified SQL query mitigates the race condition issue. @kairosci

It seems to me that the test only checks that there are no errors in the logs.
It does not verify that the DBConfig table contains only one row.
I think this should be added for greater verbosity.

> It seems to me that the test only checks that there are no errors in the logs. It does not verify that the DBConfig table contains only one row. I think this should be added for greater verbosity.

Added the check to verify only one row is created in DBConfig table. Also verified that the row count assertion is valid for this isolated state and cannot be disrupted by other tests in the same file.

> How to fix?

You can open the `Validate source code changes` test, scroll down and there you can see:

```
+ ./bin/golangci-lint run --build-tags=apparmor,seccomp,selinux
libpod/sqlite_state.go:319:1: File is not properly formatted (gofumpt)
	// Ignoring prevents a race condition where multiple Podman processes 
^
1 issues:
* gofumpt: 1
```

You can also reproduce it locally by running `make lint` or `make validate-source`.

Check that line. It most likely have a trailing whitespace.
@SanjayReddy91 thanks for the PR! after you've made the lint changes (`make validate`), please squash your commits into one.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

> @SanjayReddy91 thanks for the PR! after you've made the lint changes (`make validate`), please squash your commits into one.

Done, squashed them into one and fixed lint issue
